questionText: The masses, $X$ g, of sugar dispensed into bags by a machine are modelled by $X \sim N(250, \sigma^2)$, where the standard deviation $\sigma$ is unknown. It is found that $5\%$ of bags contain more than $260$ g. Find $\sigma$, and hence find the proportion of bags containing less than $245$ g. Give the proportion to $3$ significant figures.

steps.description: State the distribution with unknown sigma
steps.explanation: The mean is known to be $250$ but the standard deviation is not. We will use the single piece of tail information to pin down $\sigma$, then answer the follow-up question.
'Five percent contain more than $260$ g' says the upper tail beyond $260$ has probability $0.05$.
The inverse normal works from the cumulative (lower-tail) probability, so we rewrite the upper-tail statement as an area of $0.95$ below $260$.
The standardised value with $95\%$ of the area below it is $1.645$; this is where $260$ must sit in $z$-units, whatever $\sigma$ turns out to be.
Standardising $260$ gives $\frac{260-\mu}{\sigma}$, and this must equal the $z$-value $1.645$ we just found, giving one equation in the one unknown $\sigma$.
Multiplying both sides by $\sigma$ and dividing by $1.645$ isolates the standard deviation.
Evaluating the fraction gives the standard deviation of the filling process to three significant figures.
Now that $\sigma$ is known, we can answer the second part: the proportion of bags below $245$ g is $P(X < 245)$.
We convert $245$ into a $z$-score using the mean and the newly found standard deviation; it is negative because $245$ is below the mean.
The area below a negative $z$ equals one minus the area below the matching positive $z$, by symmetry of the standard normal curve.
The cumulative normal at $0.8224$ gives the fraction of the distribution below that point.
Subtracting from one gives the proportion of bags containing less than $245$ g.
This is the required proportion, about $20.5\%$ of bags.

steps.workingLatex: 245 \text{ is below the mean, so } P < 0.5
steps.explanation: Since $245$ is below the mean of $250$, we expect fewer than half of the bags to fall below it; $0.205 < 0.5$ agrees, confirming the answer is reasonable.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $\sigma \approx 6.08$ g, and the proportion below $245$ g is $P(X < 245) \approx 0.205$.
